prompt: Design a TraceCare Offline Compliance Portal to unify two on-prem operations: a clinic’s physical exam offerings and an agricultural supplier’s product traceability records, both managed under one offline site. Roles include Administrator, Clinic Staff, Catalog Manager, and End User (patient/customer). In the Vue.js interface, administrators configure physical exam packages by composing versioned exam items and setting pricing in USD, a validity window (for example, 90 days from publish), and whether each item is required or optional; staff can view “what changed” between item versions and confirm they are selling the correct edition. End users browse packages and traceability pages through a searchable catalog that supports multi-criteria filtering (status, tags, date range, priority) plus full-text search across titles and descriptions, and they can save up to 20 personal “quick views” for recurring workflows. After an order is marked completed, users can submit a review with a 1–5 star rating, up to 1,000 characters of text, up to 6 images (PNG/JPG, 5 MB each), optional tags, and one follow-up review within 14 days; the UI enforces anti-spam limits of one review attempt per order per 10 minutes and clearly labels pinned or collapsed reviews. A built-in CMS supports draft→review→publish for clinic notices and traceability education pages, optional multi-store and multilingual variants, local SEO metadata, sitemap generation for offline exports, and one-click rollback to any of the last 30 revisions. A unified Message & Reminder Center presents in-app status updates (accepted/arrived/completed/exception), conversation threads tied to an order, read/unread badges, and user-controlled subscription preferences; if masked-number relay is enabled, the UI displays only a virtual contact identifier rather than real phone numbers. The backend uses FastAPI to implement a decoupled REST architecture consumed by the Vue.js client over the local network (or localhost), with PostgreSQL as the system of record. Data models persist exam item dictionaries with reference ranges, units, applicable sex/age rules, contraindications, and collection methods; package composition stores item snapshots to guarantee historical traceability even after dictionary edits. Agricultural catalog models capture category/spec attributes such as grade, origin, harvest batch, packaging spec, and shelf life, and store attachments (test reports, licenses, plot/batch files) as locally saved files with MIME/type checks, size limits, and cryptographic file fingerprints to detect tampering without cloud scanning. Review credibility is computed transparently using a locally auditable scoring rule (for example, weighting verified completed orders at 1.0 and follow-up edits at 0.5, subtracting 0.2 for rapid repeat submissions), and moderation supports pin/collapse/sort policies per store. Notifications are strictly offline: in-app messages are persisted, retries occur at 1, 5, and 15 minutes on delivery failures within the app, and delivery metrics are reported as local success/fail counts; SMS/email sending is not used. Authentication is local username/password only, with passwords hashed using Argon2id and session tokens rotated every 12 hours; sensitive fields such as contact info are encrypted at rest and masked in logs. An admin console provides site-rule management, task management, proxy pool settings (limited to internal network routing tables, not external services), data preview/export to CSV, and system parameters; additional REST endpoints allow external on-prem systems to trigger tasks and query status, protected by role-based access control and per-key rate limits (e.g., 60 requests/minute) without requiring any internet connectivity.
